meta.title: API and GraphQL Configuration
meta.excerpt: Strapi API endpoints and GraphQL schema setup

seo.metaDescription: Strapi API endpoints and GraphQL schema setup

props.text: Complete documentation of REST and GraphQL API configuration, endpoint documentation, authentication, webhooks, and API version management.

links.title: Base URL
links.description: All REST endpoints are prefixed with /api

props.body:
Query params: ?pagination[pageSize]=10&pagination[page]=2
• Response includes: data, meta (pagination info)
• Default page size: 25 items

Basic: GET /api/articles?filters[slug][$eq]=my-article
• Contains: GET /api/articles?filters[title][$containsi]=typescript
• Range: GET /api/articles?filters[createdAt][$gte]=2026-01-01
• Multiple: GET /api/articles?filters[status][$eq]=published&filters[featured][$eq]=true&_or=1

Ascending: GET /api/articles?sort[0]=title:asc
• Descending: GET /api/articles?sort[0]=createdAt:desc
• Multiple: GET /api/articles?sort[0]=featured:desc&sort[1]=createdAt:desc

links.title: Endpoint
links.description: All GraphQL requests go to POST /graphql

props.title: Query Example
Mutation Example

props.body:
Query only fields you need
• Single request for related data
• Strongly typed schema
• Automatic schema documentation
• Better performance for mobile/slow networks

links.title: Public Access
links.description: Default: all endpoints public for GET requests

props.body:
1. Go to Strapi Admin > Settings > API Tokens
• 2. Click 'Create new API token'
• 3. Name: Frontend Application
• 4. Type: Full access or custom
• 5. Copy token and store securely

Strapi Admin > Settings > API > Rate Limiting
• Default: 100 requests per minute per IP
• Can be customized per route

X-RateLimit-Limit - Max requests allowed
• X-RateLimit-Remaining - Requests remaining
• X-RateLimit-Reset - Time until limit resets
• Status 429 Too Many Requests when limit exceeded

1. Strapi Admin > Settings > Webhooks
• 2. Create new webhook
• 3. Enter URL: https://your-domain.com/api/webhooks
• 4. Select events (create, update, delete)
• 5. Test webhook with sample payload

article.created - New article published
• article.updated - Article modified
• article.deleted - Article removed
• article.published - Article status changed

Strapi Admin > Settings > CORS
• Set allowed origins (your Next.js domain)
• Allowed methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS
• Allow credentials: true for authenticated requests
• Example: https://example.com, https://*.vercel.app
